notes: User sends a follow-up WHILE codex is mid-turn; codex's TUI queues it ('Messages to be submitted after next tool call') and folds it into the SAME turn, so the rollout records ONE task_started/task_complete pair plus a second user_message with no intervening turn boundary. The daemon must therefore see a single contiguous working span across the queued-message arrival and only flip working→ready at the single task_complete — no premature ready flicker, no waiting state. Recorded with the INTERACTIVE driver so the live REPL stays alive across the turn and the daemon observes the full working→ready arc (a headless `codex exec` is too short and exits before the flip). The mid-turn follow-up is delivered via raw `keys` steps (NOT a `send`) so the driver does not bump its expected-turn counter — codex emits only one task_complete for the folded turn, so a second wait_turn would otherwise hang. The follow-up is split into a literal-text `keys` then (after a 1s pause) a separate `keys` Enter, because codex's Ink input handler drops a batched text+Enter; an 8s pre-injection sleep ensures the follow-up lands inside the working turn (after the count begins) rather than during the ~15-20s MCP boot, where it would fold into the initial prompt with no observable queue. Daemon shape mirrors basic-turn: proc-PID pre-session born ready, then the rollout session reconcile (new session_id) born ready, then ONE working/ready flip. The contiguity invariant is enforced on first_turn_start via duration_at_least_ms (working must hold ≥8s, spanning the ~3s-in queued arrival) plus a no-waiting invariant over that forward window; first_turn_end anchors to first_turn_start so the matcher picks the single post-turn ready settle. The exact tool-call count/timing within the turn is model-dependent and not asserted.

text: Pre-session appears in ready within 1s of launch
Rollout session reconciles (different session_id), born ready
PID bound to the rollout session
Initial slow-count prompt → working; working stays contiguous for ≥8s spanning the mid-turn queued follow-up — no premature ready flicker and never enters waiting while the queued message is buffered
Cycle ends at ready only when the folded turn completes (the single task_complete); state never entered waiting
Driver tears down the REPL — process_exited on the rollout session
